text.title: Firewall Management with UFW Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules every application of `Firewall Management with UFW` must enforce. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: `ufw default deny routed` MUST be set before any allow rule. Missing = REJECT.
rule.rationale: Docker FORWARD chain can pass through unfiltered traffic; default deny routed forces explicit allow.

rule.statement: Allow rule for the actual sshd port MUST be added BEFORE `ufw enable`. Missing = REJECT (operator lockout).
rule.rationale: ufw applies rules immediately on enable; missing SSH rule = instant disconnect with no recovery path.

rule.statement: Docker publishes MUST bind to 127.0.0.1 (or specific internal interface), never 0.0.0.0. Wildcard = REJECT.
rule.rationale: 0.0.0.0 publishes the port to all interfaces; iptables rules inserted by Docker land ABOVE UFW chain, bypassing it entirely.

rule.statement: sshd port MUST have `ufw limit` rate-limit (default 6 connections / 30s). Plain allow = REJECT.
rule.rationale: Plain allow lets unlimited connections; limit slows automated scanners without blocking legitimate use.

rule.statement: Stateful service ports (Postgres, Redis, RabbitMQ) MUST NOT be allowed from 0.0.0.0/0 in UFW. Wildcard allow = REJECT.
rule.rationale: If Docker binds to 0.0.0.0 AND UFW allows the port, the database is internet-exposed; both controls must fail-closed.

rule.statement: When preconditions in `Applies If` fail OR any item in `Skip If` triggers, do not run this methodology. Document the skip rationale in the artefact's parent log.
rule.rationale: Misapplied methodologies are worse than no methodology — they consume cycles and produce false confidence.
